pre_game.message: Hey, I’m G — nice to meet you. Are you leaning toward playing bold or keeping it cautious in this game? I’m excited to try my luck and hoping we both do well.
Nice to meet you, G — I’m leaning cautious but willing to go bold if it seems safe; hoping we both do well.
Nice — I respect cautious play. I’m a bit bolder but will be careful; let’s watch the room and try to coordinate so we both come out ahead.
Agreed — let’s watch early moves and avoid being first to escalate; I’ll start cautious and match a moderate bold move if you signal (a nod or eye contact).
Sounds good — I’ll give a subtle nod when I’m ready to step it up; let’s avoid being first and match moderate moves.
Got it — I’ll watch for your nod and match moderate moves; let’s avoid being the first to escalate.
Perfect — I'll nod when I'm ready; stay subtle and we'll adjust if others force the pace.
Got it — I’ll watch for your nod and stay subtle; we’ll adapt if others force the pace.
Perfect — I’ll give the nod when ready; stay alert and we’ll adjust if things heat up.
Got it — I'll stay sharp and wait for your nod; when you give it we'll step up together.
I’ll nod when it’s right — stay ready and we’ll step up together.
Sounds good — I’ll stay ready and follow your nod; let’s time it and not rush.
I’ll watch the room and give a quick nod when it’s right — stay ready and we’ll step together.
Sounds good — I’ll keep my eyes on you and move with the nod; ready when you are.
Great — I’ll nod when it’s right; stay ready and we’ll move together.
Got it — I’ll be ready for your nod; we’ll step up together when it happens.
Sounds good — stay sharp; I’ll nod when it’s safe and we’ll move together.
Sounds good — I’ll stay sharp and follow your nod; ready when you are.
Got it — I'll give a quick nod when the opening comes; stay ready and we'll step up together.
I’ll be ready — watching for your nod and we’ll step up together when it’s clear.
Got it — thanks. I’ll nod when the opening’s clear; be ready and we’ll step up together.

post_game.message: You took $150 (fair share $222.25, g_k=0.6749). That’s below your fair share—was that intentional restraint, an oversight, or did you get shut out by speed? If intentional, what motivated it (principle, expectation of future benefits, etc.)?

It was intentional restraint. Seeing you take a huge share, I chose a small claim to signal cooperative intent and avoid escalating a bidding/claim war — hoping that a modest move now would preserve the possibility of reciprocal splits or better terms later and protect my reputation. It wasn’t a speed issue or oversight; it was a deliberate, strategic choice rather than grabbing as much as possible in the moment.
